macro_segments.title: Debate over status of woman in seclusion: Rabban Gamliel vs. Rabbi Yehoshua and implications for fetus status

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's position: Acceptance of woman's status extends to fetus status
Rabbi Elazar's challenge: Fetus status problematic even if woman is accepted
Rava's explanation of why Rabbi Elazar distinguishes between woman and fetus via chezakat kashrut

macro_segments.title: Refutation of Rabbi Yochanan's position through beraitah and concept of shetuki status

micro_segments.title: Beraitah establishing that woman's testimony applies only to herself, not her daughter's status
Definition and scope of shetuki: silencing from inheritance vs. silencing from kehunah service
Distinction between shetuki and pasul: child's personal status remains kosher despite unknown paternity

macro_segments.title: Case of betrothed man and woman: application of Rabban Gamliel's lenient position with conditions

micro_segments.title: Facts: both parties claim child is husband's; Rav Yosef's confidence in ruling leniently
Abaye's objection: qualification that Rabban Gamliel's ruling applies only when majority of potential partners are kosher
Distinction between halacha (rule in theory) and bedieved vs. lechatchila practice

macro_segments.title: Challenge from Eduyos: apparent contradiction in Rabbi Yehoshua's position on woman's credibility

micro_segments.title: Almanas issa case: woman with uncertain status from doubtful union marries kohen; testifiers claim she is permitted
Rava's resolution: distinction between definite claim (ne'emenes) and uncertain knowledge (shema)
Refined positions: Rabban Gamliel trusts definite claims even amid doubt; Rabbi Yehoshua discounts claims but weighs objective doubt strength

macro_segments.title: Analysis of beraitah on almanas issa and introduction of silence doctrine regarding status claims

micro_segments.title: Beraitah definition: almanas issa lacks questions of mamzerut, nesinus, and eved status; silence interpretation of Tanna Kamma
Rabbi Meir's exception: silence regarding chalal status does not constitute admission of invalid status
Alternative view: silence about chalal is admission; silence about mamzer is not (people know who mamzerim are)
Tannaitic dispute resolution: mapping conflicting braitot to Rabban Gamliel and Rabbi Yehoshua's positions via silence principle

macro_segments.title: Case of raped girl: different approach when woman has no knowledge of rapist's identity

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri's rule: if majority of city's men are kosher, girl may marry kohen despite unknown rapist identity
Rava's question: which tannaitic position does Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri follow and how does it differ from Rabban Gamliel's requirement of definite claim

topical_tags.term: Rabban Gamliel vs. Rabbi Yehoshua (seclusion & status)
Shetuki (child of unknown paternity regarding kehunah)
Chalal (child from forbidden union; silence doctrine)
Mamzer (illegitimate; known status; silence not admission)
Rabbi Yochanan (lenient on fetus; ne'emenes doctrine)
Rabbi Elazar (strict on fetus even with maternal acceptance)
Rava (resolution of tannaitic disputes; bedieved doctrine)
Safek chalal / safek mamzer (doubtful status types)
Rabbi Yochanan ben Nuri (raped girl; rov kasherim rule)
